title: Valid Binary Search Tree

class Solution:

	def isValidBST(self, A):

hints: Use inorder traversal to check if the tree is a valid BST.
Inorder traversal of a valid BST produces a sorted (strictly increasing) sequence.
Perform inorder traversal and collect all node values.
Check if the resulting array is strictly increasing (each element is greater than the previous).
If any element is less than or equal to the previous element, it's not a valid BST.
Alternatively, you can use recursive approach with min/max bounds for each node.
